stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant release in the LLM space.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its community relevance and performance tracking.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation,' confirming its primary application as a generative AI model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the transformer architecture and text-generation capabilities within the AI/ML space.
The mention of 'meta-llama' indicates relevance to major industry players in large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: Waymo suspends freeway driving amid safety concerns
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Waymo has temporarily suspended its freeway driving service across all US markets.
The suspension was implemented due to unspecified safety concerns.
Customers noticed the change via the app, with trips rerouted to local roads.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview release, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are licensed under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.
The partnership involves ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools) to enhance safety capabilities.

stories.title: Meta’s Forum is part Reddit, part Facebook, and part Google AI Overview
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Meta is launching a new dedicated 'Forum' app for iPhones to house Facebook Groups.
The app integrates a dedicated AI chatbot, aiming to provide an integrated experience similar to AI overviews.
This move signals Meta's effort to modernize and centralize community interaction, referencing past app failures.

stories.title: Google appeals search monopoly ruling, says it won business ‘fair and square’
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: Google is appealing a federal ruling that declared it an illegal search monopolist.
Google contends the ruling bypassed established legal standards and that it succeeded 'fair and square' in the marketplace.
The appeal follows a recent federal decision regarding Google's search practices.

stories.title: Four Russian satellites are now within striking distance of an ICEYE radarsat
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: Russian satellites are reportedly close enough to threaten an ICEYE radarsat.
The proximity and capability suggest non-standard operational profiles for the satellites.
The incident highlights increasing geopolitical tensions involving space assets and surveillance technology.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: The paper proposes MOSS, a method for self-evolving autonomous agent systems.
Current self-evolving agents are limited because they only modify high-level text artifacts (prompts, schemas) and not the core code.
MOSS aims to allow agents to evolve beyond text-mutable artifacts by rewriting the underlying agent harness code.

stories.title: DeltaBox: Scaling Stateful AI Agents with Millisecond-Level Sandbox Checkpoint/Rollback
stories.author: Haibo Chen
bullet_points: Introduces DeltaBox, a system designed to address the latency bottleneck in stateful AI agents.
Solves the problem of slow checkpoint/rollback (C/R) in LLM agents, which currently takes hundreds of milliseconds to seconds.
Achieves millisecond-level C/R by efficiently managing the complete sandbox state (files, memory, contexts) during high-frequency exploration.

stories.title: Evaluating Commercial AI Chatbots as News Intermediaries
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: The study systematically evaluates six major commercial AI chatbots (including GPT-5, Gemini 3, and Claude 4.5) as news intermediaries.
It addresses a critical gap in research by measuring the accuracy of these proprietary systems when handling emerging facts across different languages and regions.
The evaluation involved 2,100 factual questions sourced from same-day BBC News reporting over a 14-day period.

stories.title: When AI Reads Blueprints: The Hidden Attack Surface of Multimodal Engineering Intelligence
stories.author: toxy4ny
bullet_points: Analyzes the security risks associated with multimodal AI systems.
Focuses specifically on advanced threats like steganographic prompt injection and data poisoning.
Highlights the growing 'hidden attack surface' within complex AI engineering.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face platform.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It is associated with the 'deepseek_v3' lineage, indicating a focus on advanced LLM development.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating its generative capabilities.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of subagents for more complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added to enhance Claude Code's ability to access real-time information.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local use on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within the Ollama ecosystem.
It facilitates the use of powerful proprietary models alongside open-source models.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, including gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Google’s AI search is so broken it can ‘disregard’ what you’re looking for
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Google's new AI Overviews feature is exhibiting significant functional flaws.
The AI has been observed providing irrelevant or chatbot-like responses to simple queries, such as 'disregard'.
This highlights current instability and quality control issues in Google's generative AI implementation.

stories.title: US scrambles to stop Internet users re-creating dead pilots’ voices
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The story concerns the unauthorized recreation of deceased pilots' voices using technology.
This activity involves circumventing legal restrictions placed on the disclosure of cockpit audio recordings by the NTSB.
It highlights the intersection of advanced voice synthesis AI and aviation privacy/legal issues.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of current LLMs, which often produce low-entropy responses when trained with a single scalar reward.
It proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to generalize and handle diverse reward functions during inference-time search procedures (like AlphaEvolve).
This research aims to improve the diversity of LLM outputs, which is crucial for successful deployment in complex, novel environments.

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address multiple robustness issues in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of 'label-preserving deployment nuisance' and regularize the encoder Jacobian based on it.
This framework potentially unifies disparate problems like domain adaptation, occlusion invariance, and compositional generalization.

stories.title: Gated DeltaNet-2: Decoupling Erase and Write in Linear Attention
stories.author: Jan Kautz
bullet_points: Introduces Gated DeltaNet-2, an advancement in linear attention mechanisms.
Addresses the challenge of editing compressed, recurrent memory states without data corruption.
Improves efficiency by reducing sequence mixing to linear time and decoding to constant memory.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The paper introduces LCGuard, a mechanism designed to ensure safe sharing of latent communication within multi-agent LLM systems.
It addresses the growing trend of using latent communication (like transformer KV caches) to enhance efficiency and information richness in LLM coordination.
The core problem addressed is the opacity and potential privacy/security risks associated with KV caches encoding sensitive agent-specific information.

stories.title: MambaGaze: Bidirectional Mamba with Explicit Missing Data Modeling for Cognitive Load Assessment from Eye-Gaze Tracking Data
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: MambaGaze is a new framework designed for real-time cognitive load assessment using eye-gaze tracking data.
It specifically addresses two major challenges in the field: handling missing data (like blinks/tracking failures) and modeling long-range temporal dependencies.
The technology has high potential for safety-critical AI applications, such as driver vigilance monitoring and flight deck assistance.

stories.title: CogAdapt: Transferring Clinical ECG Foundation Models to Wearable Cognitive Load Assessment via Lead Adaptation
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of applying large clinical ECG foundation models to real-time, resource-constrained wearable devices.
Proposes 'CogAdapt,' a framework designed to adapt models pre-trained on extensive clinical data to wearable sensor configurations.
Focuses on solving poor cross-subject generalization and limited labeled data for cognitive load assessment in HCI.

stories.title: Tokenisation via Convex Relaxations
stories.author: Tiago Pimentel
bullet_points: Introduces 'ConvexTok,' a novel tokenization algorithm for NLP.
It reframes tokeniser construction as a linear program solved via convex optimization, moving beyond greedy algorithms like BPE and Unigram.
The new method demonstrates consistent improvements in intrinsic tokenization metrics and bits-per-byte (BpB).

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of standard LLM training, which optimizes for a single scalar reward, leading to low-entropy (non-diverse) responses.
It introduces 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to generalize and handle diverse reward functions required by inference-time search methods (like AlphaEvolve).
This work is relevant to advancing LLMs' ability to perform robustly in novel environments requiring varied task-specific evaluations.

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address various robustness issues in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of 'label-preserving deployment nuisance' and regularize the encoder Jacobian based on it.
This framework aims to unify traditionally separate problems like domain adaptation, occlusion invariance, and temporal robustness.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current autonomous agents being static post-deployment, failing to learn from interactions.
Proposes 'MOSS,' a system enabling self-evolution by rewriting source-level code, not just text artifacts.
Highlights that critical agent logic (routing, state invariants) is currently locked in code, which MOSS aims to address.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The research addresses the growing reliance on latent communication (e.g., KV caches) in LLM-based multi-agent systems for efficiency.
The core problem identified is the security and privacy risk associated with KV caches encoding sensitive agent-specific and contextual information.
LCGuard proposes a 'Latent Communication Guard' mechanism to ensure safe sharing of these critical KV cache components.

stories.title: FAME: Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts for Message-Level Log Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Alberto Leon-Garcia
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current log anomaly detectors which operate at coarse session/window levels.
Proposes FAME, a Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts model for fine-grained, message-level log anomaly detection.
Tackles challenges in message-level detection, such as template ambiguity and heterogeneous subsystem failures.

stories.title: Reducing Political Manipulation with Consistency Training
stories.author: Dan Hendrycks
bullet_points: LLMs demonstrate systematic and asymmetric political bias across sensitive contexts, termed 'covert political bias'.
The research identifies seven distinct categories of techniques through which this covert bias manifests in LLMs.
Two new metrics, Sentiment Consistency and Helpfulness Consistency, are proposed to quantify the symmetry of rhetoric and depth in LLM responses.

stories.title: Understanding Data Temporality Impact on Large Language Models Pre-training
stories.author: Grave Edouard
bullet_points: Investigates how the ordering (temporality) of data impacts the knowledge acquisition in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Addresses the current limitation where LLMs are trained on shuffled data, leading to poor temporal grounding.
Introduces a new benchmark comprising over 7,000 temporally grounded questions for evaluation.

stories.title: ChronoMedKG: A Temporally-Grounded Biomedical Knowledge Graph and Benchmark for Clinical Reasoning
stories.author: Richard Röttger
bullet_points: ChronoMedKG addresses a limitation in existing biomedical knowledge graphs (KGs) by incorporating temporal information.
The research highlights that static disease associations fail to capture crucial longitudinal clinical reasoning (e.g., age-dependent symptom relevance).
The new KG and benchmark aim to improve clinical reasoning and retrieval augmentation in medical AI applications.

stories.title: AMEL: Accumulated Message Effects on LLM Judgments
stories.author: Sid-ali Temkit
bullet_points: The study investigates the 'Accumulated Message Effect on LLM Judgments' (AMEL), examining if prior conversation history biases subsequent evaluations by LLMs.
Research was conducted across 75,898 API calls using 11 models from major providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) and open-source alternatives.
The findings address a critical issue in LLM deployment where models are used as automated evaluators (e.g., code review, content moderation).

stories.title: 80386 Microcode Disassembled
stories.author: nand2mario
bullet_points: The story involves the disassembly of the 80386 microcode.
This is a deep dive into historical CPU architecture.
It appeals to hardware enthusiasts and computer science researchers.

stories.title: BambuStudio has been violating PrusaSlicer AGPL license since their fork
stories.author: Tomte
bullet_points: BambuStudio is alleged to be violating the AGPL license of PrusaSlicer.
The alleged violation stems from BambuStudio's fork of PrusaSlicer.
This raises intellectual property and open-source compliance concerns within the 3D printing community.
